page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 12 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB per core, and is equipped with a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture with 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10%. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is also highly efficient, registering in the top 10% at 154.63 GB/sec, while large block read-only memory bandwidth is strong at 288.12 GB/sec. The system excels in single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, which both rank in the top 10%. For LLM workloads, the NVIDIA L40S GPU delivers top-tier text generation speeds of 368.94 tokens/sec for small models and strong text generation speeds of 36.32 tokens/sec for medium models. Multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall within the average range.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated 48 GB GPU, this server offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring accelerated computing without massive multi-core CPU overhead. The absence of local storage requires external storage attachments, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM text generation, image processing, and single-threaded data compression tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based instance does not include local storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and exceptional large-block memory write bandwidth. The server is highly efficient at single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression, and delivers strong performance in LLM text generation for small and medium models. This hardware profile is well-suited for machine learning inference, image processing, and memory-intensive accelerated workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM, optimized for machine learning inference and single-core workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S features 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 48 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An accelerated computing platform combining dedicated Ada Lovelace graphics architecture with high-capacity system memory for demanding inference workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
12 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processor
128 GB system memory providing 10.67 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage bundled, requiring external storage configuration
Optimized for LLM inference and image processing workloads
